title: Simplified Lasagna (Vegetarian)

subs.name: thin whole-wheat roti or tortilla layers
subs.notes: not traditional but works as a no-boil shortcut layered straight into the dish

ingredients.name: ripe tomatoes, blended, or canned crushed tomatoes

subs.name: fresh mint or coriander leaves mixed with dried oregano
subs.notes: the flavour profile shifts slightly but remains aromatic

subs.name: coconut milk or oat milk for a dairy-free béchamel
subs.notes: coconut milk adds a subtle sweetness; add extra flour if the sauce is too thin

subs.name: crumbled paneer mixed with 2 tbsp cream
subs.notes: press-dry the paneer first so it doesn't release water during baking

steps.text: Make the tomato sauce: heat olive oil in a pan, add onion and cook for 4 minutes until soft. Add garlic and fry 1 minute, then add the tomatoes, oregano, salt and half the basil. Simmer uncovered for 15 minutes until the sauce thickens.

Make the béchamel: melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Add flour and stir constantly for 1 minute until the raw flour smell disappears. Gradually pour in the milk, whisking continuously to prevent lumps.
Continue stirring the béchamel over medium heat for 5–7 minutes until thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Season with salt, black pepper and a pinch of nutmeg. Remove from heat.

If using dried lasagna sheets that are not 'no-boil', cook them in salted boiling water for 6–7 minutes until pliable but not fully cooked. Drain and lay flat on an oiled surface. Pre-boil lasagna sheets save half the baking time.

Preheat the oven to 180 °C. Spread a thin layer of tomato sauce on the base of a 30 × 20 cm baking dish. Lay a single layer of pasta sheets over it, then spoon over a layer of béchamel and scatter some grated cheese.

Repeat the layers — tomato sauce, pasta, béchamel, cheese — until all the ingredients are used, finishing with a generous layer of béchamel and a thick blanket of grated cheese on top. Tuck in the remaining basil leaves.

Cover loosely with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for a further 15–20 minutes until the top is deeply golden and the edges are bubbling. Rest for 10 minutes before cutting and serving.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: pasta → thin whole-wheat roti or tortilla layers (not traditional but works as a no-boil shortcut layered straight into the dish); milk → coconut milk or oat milk for a dairy-free béchamel (coconut milk adds a subtle sweetness; add extra flour if the sauce is too thin); cheese → crumbled paneer mixed with 2 tbsp cream (press-dry the paneer first so it doesn't release water during baking); basil → fresh mint or coriander leaves mixed with dried oregano (the flavour profile shifts slightly but remains aromatic)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Baking the lasagna requires an oven. Without one, assemble the layers in a deep kadai or pressure-cooker insert, cover tightly and cook on the lowest flame for 25–30 minutes — it will not brown on top but will cook through. Use Amul butter and Amul mozzarella (sold in blocks in most cities) or any processed cheese. Dried lasagna sheets are available at gourmet grocery stores or online; look for 'instant lasagna' which requires no pre-boiling.
